rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF TOMMY HUANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (6806 Gunn Dr, Oakland, Ca)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The reported sale appears to have been made (offer accepted and returned to Court for confirmation) in September 2022, BEFORE notice of sale was published in February 2023, and thus failed to comply with Probate Code section 10306(a); see also LRSF 14.31B. The notice of sale published in February 2023 did not state the date on or after which the sale will be made and thus failed to comply with section 10304(a)(2). 2) The purchase contract identifies the buyer as "Ed Hemmat", but the petition and proof of service of notice of this sale identified him as "Ed Hammat". 3) Based on the most recent reappraisal on file, the sale price must be at least $76,900 (= 90% of the reappraised value), but the reported sale price is $59,000 and thus does not satisfy the requirement of section 10309(a)(3). 4) The minimum overbid amount must be calculated based on 100% ownership of the subject real property under LRSF 14.32(G), even though the Court only confirms the sale of the estate's 50% ownership. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF SUEKO HONIG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/05/2023 (no earlier date available), as nothing has been filed to address the following issues e-mailed to counsel on 02/23/2023: 1) The bond has not been increased. 2) No corrected inventory and appraisal (referred to in paragraph 3 of the amended petition) has been filed. 3) No original statement of the Union Bank account #8309 verifying the 11/17/20 balance is on file. 4) The total charges do not match the total credits. 5) Schedule A-1 shows distribution of $50,612.67 received from the deceased spouse's conservator, but Schedule C-1 to the deceased spouse's conservator's supplemental accounting shows distribution of $50,689.71 to this conservatorship. 6) Please state the purpose of the $90 payment to Kie Taylor on 1/6/21 reported on Schedule C. 7) Schedule C subtotals do not match the total amount of disbursements. 8) The petitioner's services between 9/4/20 and 11/16/20 described on Exhibit B appear to be related to the conservatorship of the person, rather than the conservatorship of the estate. An appearance may be required on 06/05/2023 on the sole issue of whether the petition should be taken off calendar if no satisfactory progress is shown.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARVIN HONIG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/05/2023 (no earlier date available), as nothing has been filed to address the following issues e-mailed to counsel on 02/23/2023: 1) No corrected inventory and appraisal (referred to in paragraph 3 of the amended petition) has been filed. 2) No original statement of the Union Bank account #8309 verifying the 11/17/20 balance is on file. 3) No original statement of the Union Bank account #8309 verifying the 1/31/22 balance is on file. The statement filed in the spouse's conservatorship case shows a difference balance. 4) Schedule C-1 does not show SSA/VA/pension payments reported to have been distributed to the spouse's conservator on 12/3/20, 12/31/20, 2/1/21, 2/3/21, 3/1/21 & 3/3/21. See Schedule A & Schedule A-1 to the spouse's conservator's accounting. 5) The petitioner's services between 9/4/20 and 11/16/20 described on Exhibit B appear to be related to the conservatorship of the person, rather than the conservatorship of the estate. 6) No spousal property order appears to have been issued for the Union Bank account #8216, Bank of America account, US Bank account, and East West Bank account. An appearance may be required on 06/05/2023 on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar if no satisfactory progress is shown.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SARAH ANN CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing in order for the heirs to receive their distribution finally, although copies of responsive papers were filed AFTER the deadline by when they were to have been submitted to the assigned examiner, and the specific assets (number of shares of each security) distributable to each heir were never set forth. Any pleading that REPLACES an allegation in a petition is an AMENDMENT, which requires notice. [CRC 7.3 and 7.53(a).] When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID JAMES LIPMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/15/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The sole heir died on 1/28/23. His agent under a power of attorney no longer has standing to represent him/his interest. A personal representative must be appointed for his estate; he presumably had a pour-over will to his trust. (2) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (There is no need to explain in a pleading that proof of service of notice was filed; the proof of service should simply be filed.) If an executor (or special administrator) is not appointed in time to either receive or waive notice for this amended petition hearing on 5/15/23, Counsel should contact the assigned examiner to request a further continuance. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/4/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TONKIN GRANDCHILDRENS TRUST OF 1999 FBO ASHTON TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To A)Establish Existence, Terms, And Validity Of Trust; B)Confirm Assets Of The Trust; And C) Appoint Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/15/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No supplement has been filed yet to set forth the address of the beneficiary. [CRC 7.902.] (2) Notice mailed on 3/7/23 for this hearing on 4/3/23 was not sufficient notice for a trust petition. Item 5 of the proof of service of the Notice form DE-115 was also not checked, indicating that a copy of the petition was served with the petition to determine claim to property mailed directly to the sole beneficiary of this trust. (3) If Ashton Finegold is an adult, he could waive notice. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/4/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CONTRERAS FAMILY TRUST, DATED MAY 13, 2019
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order That: 1) Removes Co-Trustee Emeline A. Contreras-Forrest; 2) Fixes The Compensation Of Trutee At Zero; 3) Confirms The
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/22/2023 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) Re the DE-120 form: i) Item 4a of the proof of service must be completed. ii) The day of signing the proof of service must be provided. iii) The proof of service must be signed. iv) Direct notice must be given to Emeline Contreras-Forrest and Sonia Lacchini. CRC 7.51(a)(1) & (2). See also Probate Code section 1215(a)(3). 2) No Schedule 1 was attached to the petition. 3) Please provide specific examples of the day-to-day activity of the trust carried on within San Francisco County by Emeline Contreras-Forrest or Antonina Contreras-Wong as trustee. 4) Please provide a copy of the settlor's financial power of attorney if available. 5) Is there a conservatorship proceeding pending or anticipated for the settlor? If not, should the Court appoint a guardian ad litem for the settlor? Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
